summary: Newton's second law ties force, mass and acceleration together: the net force on an object equals its mass times its acceleration. It is the central tool for any problem about why motion changes.
method: Identify the object and add up all forces on it to get the net force F.
Decide which quantity is unknown — force, mass, or acceleration.
Rearrange F = m·a for that unknown (a = F/m or m = F/a).
Put every quantity in SI units (kg, m/s², N) before substituting.
Substitute and compute; check the direction of the acceleration matches the net force.

example.title: Accelerating a shopping cart
steps: The unknown is the force, so use F = m·a directly.
Substitute the values: F = 5 kg · 2 m/s².
